steps.title: Identify plural nouns
steps.content: The sentence refers to placing both hands between two antennas. Hands and antennas are simple plural nouns here; neither noun owns anything, so neither should have an apostrophe.

steps.title: Reject false possessives
steps.content: Choice A incorrectly uses possessive forms for hand and antenna. Choice C makes both plural nouns possessive. Choice D still makes hands possessive even though hands is just the object of place.

steps.title: Use plain plural forms
steps.content: Hands between the two antennas uses ordinary plurals with no apostrophes, which matches the grammar and meaning of the sentence. The answer is B.
